market.advice: Pre-open with the uptrend intact but narrowing: SPY $750.7 sits above its 50-day (742.8), only -0.9% off its high, breadth healthy at 66% above the 50-day and VIX calm at 18.0 (though ticking up 5-day). The clear tape message is ROTATION — money is leaving mega-cap tech, semiconductors and high-multiple growth (NVDA, MU, INTC, ORCL, TSLA, most quantum/space names all consolidating or breaking down; QQQ is back below its 50-day) and flowing into transports/industrials, banks & insurers, REITs, utilities, consumer staples and defensive healthcare. Into the open, favor the leadership groups on clean, un-extended setups; keep normal position sizing while the state stays green; and don't chase names already overbought (WERN, CSX, NTRS, CROX, AAPL, SIRI all closed hot). Honor stops on the lagging tech/semi complex rather than averaging down.

picks.thesis: Top scorer on the board and the cleanest staples setup — Monster closed at a fresh 52-week high with RSI only 65 and +33% 3-month momentum, a 4-contraction VCP base tightening to 2.6%. Defensive growth (EPS +29%, revenue +27%) is exactly what a rotation-driven tape rewards. TradingView independently rates it Strong Buy, confirming the breakout.
strengths: Tight VCP base (4 contractions, 2.6%) — low-risk entry
risks: Earnings in ~21 days (Aug 7) — event risk builds into the entry
Fwd P/E ~38 leaves little room for a growth miss

picks.thesis: Best-in-class intermodal/trucking operator at a fresh 52-week high (RSI 66, +25% 3-month) as the freight cycle inflects and transports lead the industrial rotation. EPS +46% off the trough. TradingView Strong Buy confirms the technical read.
strengths: New high with room on RSI (66)
risks: Freight demand is macro-cyclical
High-beta transport — first to sell off on a market pullback

picks.thesis: IDEX broke to new highs on a tight base with RSI only 53 — the least-extended top-scorer, so the most room to run. Diversified industrial fluidics/pumps with durable margins; a lower-beta way to own the industrial leadership.
strengths: New high with un-extended RSI (53) — most room on the list
risks: TradingView rates it Buy (not Strong Buy) — slightly softer confirmation
Short-cycle industrial demand can soften quickly

picks.thesis: Federal Realty at new highs with RSI only 56 — least-extended of the REIT leaders. Best-in-class retail centers with the longest dividend-growth record in the group; REITs are leading on the softer-rate outlook. TradingView Strong Buy.
strengths: Highest-quality open-air retail REIT, durable dividend growth
risks: A rate back-up would pressure the whole REIT group
Retail-tenant credit sensitivity in a slowdown

picks.thesis: Citizens Financial broke to new highs (RSI 63, EPS +47%) with TradingView rating it Strong Buy — a regional bank confirming the financials' breadth well beyond the money-centers. Clean, un-extended participation in the leadership group.
risks: Regionals carry CRE-exposure headline risk
Deposit-cost pressure if rates stay elevated

picks.thesis: W.W. Grainger at new highs (RSI 57, +24% 3-month) — MRO distribution is a steady industrial compounder and TradingView rates it Strong Buy. Un-extended for a name printing highs.
strengths: New high with only mid-range RSI (57)
risks: High absolute share price — size positions carefully
Industrial capex slowdown risk

picks.thesis: Public Storage just -1.6% off its high with RSI only 53 — an un-extended, defensive self-storage REIT with a fortress balance sheet participating in the rate-relief REIT bid. TradingView Strong Buy.
risks: Storage demand softens if housing turnover stays frozen
Rate back-up would pressure REITs

picks.thesis: Dominion Energy at a fresh high (RSI 63) as regulated utilities catch a bid on the softer-rate outlook and data-center power demand. Revenue +23% with a rate base benefiting from load growth. TradingView Strong Buy.
strengths: Regulated utility leveraged to data-center load growth
risks: EPS still negative on the trailing read (regulatory/rate lag)
Rate-sensitive; a yield back-up caps upside

picks.thesis: Aflac firm near highs (RSI 65) — a lower-beta insurer offering financial-sector leadership exposure with steady underwriting and heavy buybacks. TradingView Strong Buy confirms.
strengths: Lower-beta way to own the financials bid
risks: Yen/FX translation exposure (large Japan book)
Slower growth than the banks

picks.thesis: Coca-Cola at new highs (RSI 64) — the anchor defensive staple as money rotates toward stability. Pricing power and global volume growth (EPS +18%, revenue +12%). TradingView Strong Buy.
risks: FX headwinds on a strong dollar
Modest growth — a valuation re-rate, not a growth story

picks.thesis: Eli Lilly just -5% off its high (RSI 57, +30% 3-month) with the strongest fundamentals on the board — EPS +170%, revenue +56% on the GLP-1 franchise. Held by a user; a core defensive-growth compounder. TradingView Buy.
strengths: Best fundamental growth on the list (EPS +170%, rev +56%)
risks: High absolute price and premium multiple
GLP-1 competition and manufacturing-capacity headlines

picks.thesis: Goldman is a core holding for several users and sits clean: RSI 55, only -5% off its high, FY EPS +92% / revenue +42% on the capital-markets reopening. TradingView confirms with a Buy; no near-term earnings event risk.
risks: Capital-markets revenue is cyclical
A rate/credit shock would pressure the group

picks.thesis: Starbucks broke to a new high (RSI 63) as the turnaround gains traction — EPS +33% with same-store traffic stabilizing. A consumer name with idiosyncratic self-help rather than pure cyclicality. TradingView Strong Buy.
risks: China same-store recovery still uneven
Consumer discretionary softness if the economy slows

picks.thesis: EOG Resources -7% off its high (RSI 58) at a cheap ~9.5x forward P/E — a best-in-class, low-cost shale operator giving the portfolio energy diversification with a big free-cash-flow yield. EPS +40%. TradingView Buy.
strengths: Cheapest pick on the list (fwd P/E ~9.5)
Low-cost operator, strong FCF and capital return
Energy diversification away from crowded tech
risks: Directly levered to the oil price — commodity risk
Not yet at new highs; needs crude to cooperate

picks.thesis: Simon Property at a new high (RSI 55) — the premier mall/retail REIT participating in the rate-relief bid with class-A assets and a rising dividend. Held by a user. Revenue +19%. TradingView Strong Buy.
strengths: Class-A retail REIT, durable and growing dividend
risks: Retail-tenant credit sensitivity in a slowdown
Rate back-up would pressure the group

picks.thesis: U.S. Bancorp at new highs (RSI 68) with TradingView rating it Strong Buy — a super-regional confirming the bank leadership on a steeper curve and healthy credit. Clean breakout with fee-income diversification.
strengths: Super-regional leverage to a steeper curve
risks: RSI 68 — getting warm, buy strength not a chase
NIM sensitivity to rate cuts

picks.thesis: JPMorgan just -1% off its high (RSI 59) — the highest-quality money-center bank with EPS +47% / revenue +30%, a fortress balance sheet and best-in-class returns. TradingView Buy confirms the financials leadership.
risks: Capital-markets and credit both cyclical
A hard-landing scenario would lift loan-loss provisions

picks.thesis: NNN REIT broke to new highs (RSI 68) as rate-sensitive triple-net REITs lead on the softer-rate outlook. Durable, growing income from a diversified net-lease book. TradingView Strong Buy confirms.
risks: Rate back-up would pressure the whole group
Tenant-credit concentration risk

picks.thesis: Corteva firm near highs (RSI 65) — a defensive ag-inputs (seeds/crop-protection) leader adding materials-sector diversification. Steady mid-teens EPS/revenue growth. TradingView Strong Buy.
risks: Farm-income and crop-price cyclicality
Weather and input-cost swings

picks.thesis: Werner is the strongest transport by momentum (+42% 3-month, new high) confirming the freight-cycle turn, but RSI 69 is running hot — prefer a pullback toward $44 rather than chasing the extension. TradingView still rates it Strong Buy.
risks: RSI 69 — extended; entry set below market to avoid chasing
High-beta trucking; sharp mean-reversion risk

picks.thesis: CSX at a new high but RSI 78 — the rails are confirming the transport leadership yet this is the most overbought of the group. A rail compounder worth owning on a pullback toward $48-49, not at a stretched high.
strengths: Rail leadership confirms the transport theme
risks: RSI 78 — overbought, high pullback risk
Rail volumes tied to industrial/coal/intermodal demand

picks.thesis: Northern Trust is a high-quality trust/custody bank (EPS +43%) riding the financials bid, but RSI 73 near its high is overbought. Wait for a cooldown toward $182 rather than paying up. TradingView Buy.
strengths: Fee-based custody/wealth model, lower credit risk
risks: RSI 73 — overbought; entry set below market
Custody revenue tied to market levels and flows

picks.thesis: Crocs at a new high with +36% 3-month momentum, but RSI 72 is overbought and EPS/revenue are slightly negative — the move is multiple-driven, not earnings-driven. TradingView Strong Buy, but wait for a pullback and watch the fundamentals confirm.
risks: RSI 72 overbought; EPS -4% / revenue -2% — fundamentals lagging the price
Footwear demand and HeyDude drag are discretionary risks

picks.thesis: DexCom is rebuilding — up strongly (RSI 69, +27% 3-month, EPS +92%) but still -13% off its high, so it's a recovering base rather than a fresh breakout. Strong CGM growth; let it clear resistance. TradingView Strong Buy.
risks: Still -13% off its high — base not yet complete
CGM competition and reimbursement headlines

picks.thesis: Fortinet is a rare tech leader in this tape — near its high (only -3.6% off) with a massive +95% 3-month momentum run and EPS +29%. Held by a user. The momentum is extended, so prefer adding on a dip toward $155. TradingView Buy.
strengths: +95% 3-month momentum — a tech name still working
risks: Momentum extended; entry set below market
Fwd P/E ~47 — priced for continued execution

picks.thesis: Garmin is a durable, cash-rich hardware compounder rated Strong Buy by TradingView, but RSI 70 and -5.8% off its high with flat 3-month momentum argue for patience — buy the next pullback toward $245.
risks: RSI 70 — overbought; 3-month momentum flat (-4.6%)
Consumer-hardware demand cyclicality

picks.thesis: Fair Isaac has strong fundamentals (EPS +69%, revenue +39%) and TradingView Buy, but the stock is -34% off its high rebuilding a deep base — a recovery candidate, not a breakout. Held by a user. Own it for the franchise, size for the volatility.
risks: -34% off high — deep base, higher volatility
Mortgage-score pricing scrutiny headline risk

picks.thesis: CVS Health at a new high with a huge +40% 3-month run and EPS +63% as the turnaround takes hold — defensive healthcare catching the rotation bid. The move is extended, so prefer a pullback toward $103. TradingView Buy.
risks: +40% 3-month — extended; entry below market
Aetna medical-loss-ratio and PBM policy risk

picks.thesis: NEW LISTING — SpaceX (SPCX) is a recently-IPO'd name held by two users, so breakout/VCP/RS signals are still blank (only weeks of history) and TradingView won't resolve a rating yet; that's expected. The thesis is fundamental: the dominant launch/Starlink franchise with unmatched cadence and a fast-growing satellite-broadband subscriber base, offset by an extreme post-IPO valuation and lockup/volatility risk. Trade small and let a base form before adding.
strengths: Starlink subscriber and revenue growth inflecting
Scarcity value as the marquee new space listing
risks: New listing — no technical base, quant signals blank, no TradingView rating yet
Extreme valuation and post-IPO/lockup volatility
Currently -38% off its brief post-IPO high — momentum negative
